top_signals.title: Oil Edges Lower as Iran Waiver Signals Peace Talks Progress
top_signals.summary: Oil edged lower as traders gauged signs of progress toward an agreement to end the Iran war, which included a US waiver allowing some sales of crude and fuels from the Islamic Republic.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: U.S. fights with Brazil for China's giant soybean market
top_signals.summary: The U.S. Soybean Export Council makes claims to higher quality a selling point over Brazil's crop, as American farmers attempt to win back business from the world's largest importer
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The Jones Act Waiver of 2026: Implications for American Shipping, Energy, and National Security
top_signals.summary: On March 17, 2026, the U.S. Department of Homeland Security took the extraordinary step of issuing a sweeping waiver of the Jones Act at the request of the U.S. Department of Defense. The waiver, one of the broadest in the history of the nation’s cabotage laws, opened U.S.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: Petrobras Orders Four New Tankers in $427M Fleet Renewal Push
top_signals.summary: Brazil's state-controlled oil company Petrobras has signed a $427 million contract through its logistics subsidiary Petrobras Transporte (Transpetro) with the Rio Grande Shipyard for the construction of four medium range tankers as part of a…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Japan top court upholds order to dissolve Unification Church
top_signals.summary: The ruling stated that the dissolution order "does not violate the Constitution, which guarantees freedom of religion and association."
